skill.name: neurokit
skill.description: Comprehensive biosignal processing for ECG/PPG/EEG/EDA/RSP/EMG/EOG; use when you need to clean, segment, and extract physiological features for HRV, event-related responses, complexity metrics, or multimodal psychophysiology pipelines.
skill.summary: Biosignal processing toolkit for ECG, PPG, EEG, EDA, RSP, EMG, and EOG data analysis.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 1156 static findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives limited to documentation files. The 1061 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' alerts refer to Python code examples inside Markdown code fences, not executable shell commands. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' references appear in JSON audit data and documentation text, not executable cryptographic code. Hardcoded URLs point to legitimate GitHub repositories. No executable scripts, dynamic code execution, or data exfiltration patterns were found. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Ruby/shell backtick execution in Markdown documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged backtick characters in Markdown code fences as Ruby/shell command execution. All occurrences are Python code examples inside markdown ```python code blocks in reference documentation files. No actual shell execution occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are Markdown documentation files containing Python code in fenced code blocks. No Ruby or shell code exists in these files.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm references
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: References to weak cryptographic algorithms appear in JSON audit result data and documentation text, not in executable cryptographic code. These are references in documentation, not actual cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No actual cryptographic code exists in this skill. All flagged text is in data files and documentation references, not in executable source code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Hardcoded URLs point to legitimate GitHub repositories (neuropsychology/NeuroKit and aipoch/medical-research-skills). These are source references, not malicious endpoints or data exfiltration targets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are well-known public GitHub repositories for the neurokit2 project. No credential leakage or data exfiltration patterns.

low_findings.title: System and network reconnaissance references in documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Text containing system or network-related terms appears in documentation examples and parameter descriptions. These are natural language references in educational documentation, not reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Terms appear in educational documentation describing signal processing methods, not in executable reconnaissance scripts.

low_findings.title: Certificate/key file pattern in documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The pattern at SKILL.md:111 is Python code printing analysis results, not an actual certificate or key file. Static analyzer likely triggered on a false pattern match.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 111 of SKILL.md contains 'print' statement outputting analysis results, not a certificate file.

low_findings.title: C2 keywords in HRV documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged 'C2 keywords' at references/hrv.md:242-243 are variable names and function parameters in Python code examples for HRV analysis, not command-and-control indicators.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: HRV analysis code uses legitimate variable naming conventions. No C2 communication patterns present.

low_findings.title: DANGEROUS COMBINATION heuristic finding
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The heuristic flagged a combination of code execution patterns (documentation code examples) and network references (GitHub URLs). Both are legitimate documentation content with no actual executable threat.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The 'dangerous combination' is an aggregation of false positives - all individual patterns are benign documentation content.

content.user_title: Analyze Biosignals with NeuroKit2 for Physiological Research
content.value_statement: Researchers struggle to process complex physiological signals from multiple recording modalities. This skill provides comprehensive biosignal processing for ECG, PPG, EEG, EDA, RSP, EMG, and EOG data using the NeuroKit2 library.
actual_capabilities: Process ECG and PPG signals for R-peak detection, heart rate calculation, and cardiac phase determination
Compute heart rate variability metrics across time, frequency, and nonlinear domains
Analyze EEG signals for band power extraction, microstate segmentation, and complexity measures
Decompose EDA signals into tonic and phasic components with SCR detection
Process respiratory signals for breathing rate and respiratory variability analysis
Perform multimodal biosignal processing with unified outputs for integrated analyses
limitations: Requires the neurokit2 Python library to be installed in the environment
Signal processing quality depends on input data quality and sampling rate
Analysis is limited to the methods implemented in the neurokit2 library
Does not provide real-time processing capabilities for live data streams

use_cases.title: Cardiovascular Health Monitoring
use_cases.description: Process ECG recordings to detect R-peaks, calculate heart rate, and compute HRV metrics for cardiovascular health assessment.

use_cases.title: Cognitive Neuroscience Experiments
use_cases.description: Analyze EEG signals for band power analysis, event-related potentials, and complexity measures in cognitive research studies.

use_cases.title: Stress and Arousal Research
use_cases.description: Decompose EDA signals to quantify sympathetic nervous system activity through tonic and phasic component analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Basic ECG Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Load an ECG signal from a CSV file and process it using the neurokit2 skill. Clean the signal, detect R-peaks, calculate heart rate, and compute basic HRV metrics.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user processing a single ECG recording

prompt_templates.title: Multimodal Biosignal Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: I have simultaneous recordings of ECG, EDA, and RSP signals. Process all three modalities together using the multimodal pipeline and provide integrated analysis results.

prompt_templates.title: EEG Band Power Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Process an EEG signal to extract band power features for delta, theta, alpha, beta, and gamma frequency bands. Provide the power values for each band.

prompt_templates.title: Event-Related Epoching Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a continuous biosignal recording with event triggers. Find the events, create epochs around each event, compute the grand average, and extract event-related features.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user running a full event-related analysis pipeline

output_examples.input: Process an ECG signal to detect heart rate and compute HRV metrics
output: HRV frequency-domain: LF 0.28, HF 0.15, LF/HF ratio 1.87
Quality assessment: 95% of beats classified as normal

output_examples.input: Decompose an EDA signal and detect skin conductance responses
output: Average rise time: 1.8 seconds, recovery time: 4.2 seconds

best_practices: Always provide the sampling_rate parameter to ensure accurate frequency-domain calculations and proper filtering.
Inspect the quality of processed signals visually before interpreting extracted features, especially with noisy recordings.
Use the multimodal bio_process pipeline when you have multiple synchronized signals for consistent integrated analysis.
anti_patterns: Do not use default sampling rates without verification - incorrect sampling rate leads to invalid frequency-domain results.
Do not apply HRV analysis on recordings shorter than 60 seconds as time-domain metrics become unreliable.
Do not ignore signal quality flags and artifact detection results when interpreting physiological features.

faq.question: What is NeuroKit2 and what can it do?
faq.answer: NeuroKit2 is a Python library for processing physiological signals including ECG, EEG, EDA, PPG, RSP, EMG, and EOG. It provides cleaning, peak detection, feature extraction, and analysis functions.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the neurokit2 Python library must be installed. You can install it using pip or uv: uv pip install neurokit2

faq.question: What types of data can this skill process?
faq.answer: This skill can process ECG, PPG, EEG, EDA, RSP, EMG, and EOG signals. It also supports multimodal analysis with synchronized recordings.

faq.question: How does the skill handle different sampling rates?
faq.answer: You must provide the sampling rate of your recording as a parameter. The skill uses this for filtering, frequency analysis, and accurate feature calculation.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze event-related data?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports event finding, epoching, baseline correction, and averaging across trials for event-related analysis workflows.

faq.question: What is the difference between event-related and interval-related analysis?
faq.answer: Event-related analysis processes short signal segments locked to specific events. Interval-related analysis processes longer continuous recordings to compute summary metrics like HRV over time windows.
